columns.name: definition
distinct_values: `1` = user's OWN brand, `0` = competitor brand in catalog
Daily sum of base revenue per grouping
First scrape timestamp for the SKU
Product type (Trim, Flower Bulk, Waste, etc.)
Purpose: Brand registry — own brand + competitor catalog Data grain: One row per (tenantid, name) — unique Use when: Identifying own brand, listing brand catalog
Purpose: Current on-hand inventory snapshot Data grain: One row per (productName, tenant) Use when: Low stock queries, what's in stock, on-hand units by product

Purpose: Flat export/snapshot of sales data synced from Google Sheets via `rhize_sync_log` Data grain: One row per order line item (similar to `rhize_orders` but all TEXT) Use when: Only when `rhize_orders` does not have the required data. Prefer `rhize_orders` for order queries Avoid when: General order queries — use `rhize_orders` (proper types, tenant-scoped)
Purpose: Lot tracking and lab result metadata Data grain: One row per (lotNumber, tenant) Use when: THC by strain, expiring lots, lot metadata, lab results
Purpose: Maps raw category strings to normalized category labels Data grain: One row per raw category variant Use when: Normalizing `category` in market tables — preferred over inline CASE block

Purpose: Maps tenants to their US state(s) — drives RLS on PostgreSQL market tables Data grain: One row per (tenantid, state_key) Use when: Understanding which state(s) a tenant operates in. Not typically queried directly in business queries

Purpose: Partner store roster (minimal version) Data grain: One row per partner customer per tenant Use when: Simple partner customer name list only. For richer data use `rhize_stores WHERE isPartner = 1`
Purpose: Per-store breakdown of tenant's products Data grain: One row per (date, Product_Name, Company_Name) per tenant Use when: Store-level product breakdown

Purpose: Pre-aggregated market data — compressed daily snapshot Data grain: One row per (date, brand, company, category, state) per day Use when: Top brands, top companies, category mix, market share, multi-day trends, any GROUP BY brand/company/category Avoid when: SKU-level detail needed (`product_name`, individual `thc`, `flag`, `days_on_shelf`)

Purpose: Raw scraped competitor/market listings Data grain: One row per (date, state, company, product, unit) per scrape run Use when: SKU-level detail needed: `product_name`, `price`, `thc`, `flag`, `days_on_shelf` Avoid when: Aggregating by brand/company/category — use `chatbot_mv_market_daily` (~480x smaller)
Purpose: Retail store / account registry Data grain: One row per store per tenant Use when: Partner stores, retail accounts, store directory
Purpose: Sales action queue / CRM follow-up tasks Data grain: One row per sales action per tenant Use when: Open tasks, CRM queue, follow-ups, sales pipeline
Purpose: Strain genetics and flavor metadata Data grain: One row per strain per tenant Use when: Strain genetics, flavor profiles, effect tags
Purpose: Tenant's OWN daily sales/revenue summary Data grain: One row per (Product_Name, date, tenant) approx. Use when: "My revenue", "my sales", daily totals, own top products, own product flags
Purpose: Tracks Google Sheets → `Rhize_sales_data` sync job results Use when: Debugging sync failures only. Never use for business analytics
Purpose: User's own product order history Data grain: One row per order line item Use when: Own orders, customer revenue, sales by product, open balances, AR
Real DATE type — arithmetic works directly
Retail store name on the order
Same 58 raw variants as base table
top_values: Purpose: Per-store breakdown of tenant's products Data grain: One row per (date, Product_Name, Company_Name) per tenant Use when: Store-level product breakdown
Purpose: Tenant's OWN daily sales/revenue summary Data grain: One row per (Product_Name, date, tenant) approx. Use when: "My revenue", "my sales", daily totals, own top products, own product flags
Purpose: Lot tracking and lab result metadata Data grain: One row per (lotNumber, tenant) Use when: THC by strain, expiring lots, lot metadata, lab results

Purpose: Maps tenants to their US state(s) — drives RLS on PostgreSQL market tables Data grain: One row per (tenantid, state_key) Use when: Understanding which state(s) a tenant operates in. Not typically queried directly in business queries
Retail store name on the order
Purpose: Current on-hand inventory snapshot Data grain: One row per (productName, tenant) Use when: Low stock queries, what's in stock, on-hand units by product
Purpose: Brand registry — own brand + competitor catalog Data grain: One row per (tenantid, name) — unique Use when: Identifying own brand, listing brand catalog
Same 58 raw variants as base table

columns.name: restrictions
distinct_values: Always `CAST(Revenue AS DECIMAL(10,2))`. This is tenant's OWN revenue — NOT market revenue
Always filter: `brand IS NOT NULL AND brand <> ''` when grouping
Always filter: `company IS NOT NULL AND company <> ''` when grouping
Always filter status for revenue vs. open orders
ALWAYS normalize. Preferred: `JOIN category_map ON lower(category) = raw_lower`. Fallback: inline CASE block (see §4). NEVER write `category = 'Flower'`
ALWAYS normalize via `category_map` join or CASE block
Always use `remaining` not `current_qty` for sellable stock
Always `WHERE days_on_shelf IS NOT NULL` when sorting/aggregating. ASC = fastest moving; DESC = stale
Default ranking metric for market aggregates
Different from market `thc` (TEXT). Always `WHERE thc IS NOT NULL` when averaging. No cast needed
Direct MySQL date arithmetic: `WHERE date >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL 30 DAY)`
Do NOT scope by `brand_id` — collides cross-tenant
Expiring soon: `WHERE expirationDate BETWEEN CURRENT_DATE AND DATE_ADD(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL 30 DAY)`
Filter blanks when ranking. Only available in base table (not in view)
Filter: `brand IS NOT NULL AND brand <> ''`
Filter: `company IS NOT NULL AND company <> ''`
Inconsistent format. Never use directly for math
Last 7d: `WHERE date >= DATE_FORMAT(CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 7 DAY, '%Y-%m-%d')`. Latest: `WHERE date = (SELECT MAX(date) FROM rhize_dataset_main)`
Low stock: `WHERE current_qty < N`. Order `ASC` for lowest first
Maps tenant to state for RLS
Must cast: `CASE WHEN thc ~ '^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?%?$' THEN regexp_replace(thc,'[^0-9.]','','g')::numeric END AS thc_num` then filter `thc_num BETWEEN 0 AND 100`
Note: column name is `customer` not `customerName` — different from `rhize_orders`
Not the business date — use `date` for business logic
No visible `tenantid` — scope may be implied by sync source
`ORDER BY priority ASC` for most urgent first
RLS: State is auto-filtered. Never add `WHERE state = ...`
Same date rules as `rhize_dataset_main`: use `DATE_FORMAT` for arithmetic
`SUM(openBalance)` for total AR. Filter `status <> 'Completed'` for outstanding
`SUM(subtotal)` for total revenue. Combine `WHERE status = 'Completed'` for realized revenue

Text, not DATE. Use `to_char()` for date arithmetic. Latest: `WHERE date = (SELECT MAX(date) FROM complete_market_scrapper_dataset)`. Last N days: `WHERE date >= to_char(CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 'N days', 'YYYY-MM-DD')`
Today's new SKUs: `WHERE Flag = 'Added' AND date = (SELECT MAX(date)...)`
Use `flag = 'added'` for new products, `flag = 'removed'` for dropped
Use for grouping all lines of one order
Use `lower(customerName) LIKE lower('%X%')` for partial match. Group by for top customers
Use `SUM(sku_count)` when grouping by brand for "most products listed"
Use this as the display/group-by value
top_values: RLS: State is auto-filtered. Never add `WHERE state = ...`
`SUM(openBalance)` for total AR. Filter `status <> 'Completed'` for outstanding
Always `WHERE days_on_shelf IS NOT NULL` when sorting/aggregating. ASC = fastest moving; DESC = stale
ALWAYS normalize. Preferred: `JOIN category_map ON lower(category) = raw_lower`. Fallback: inline CASE block (see §4). NEVER write `category = 'Flower'`
Today's new SKUs: `WHERE Flag = 'Added' AND date = (SELECT MAX(date)...)`
Do NOT scope by `brand_id` — collides cross-tenant
Use for grouping all lines of one order
Low stock: `WHERE current_qty < N`. Order `ASC` for lowest first
Use `flag = 'added'` for new products, `flag = 'removed'` for dropped
Not the business date — use `date` for business logic
Always filter: `company IS NOT NULL AND company <> ''` when grouping
Note: column name is `customer` not `customerName` — different from `rhize_orders`

Text, not DATE. Use `to_char()` for date arithmetic. Latest: `WHERE date = (SELECT MAX(date) FROM complete_market_scrapper_dataset)`. Last N days: `WHERE date >= to_char(CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 'N days', 'YYYY-MM-DD')`
Always filter status for revenue vs. open orders
Use this as the display/group-by value
ALWAYS normalize via `category_map` join or CASE block
`SUM(subtotal)` for total revenue. Combine `WHERE status = 'Completed'` for realized revenue
Last 7d: `WHERE date >= DATE_FORMAT(CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 7 DAY, '%Y-%m-%d')`. Latest: `WHERE date = (SELECT MAX(date) FROM rhize_dataset_main)`
Filter: `brand IS NOT NULL AND brand <> ''`
Always `CAST(Revenue AS DECIMAL(10,2))`. This is tenant's OWN revenue — NOT market revenue
Always use `remaining` not `current_qty` for sellable stock
sample_values: RLS: State is auto-filtered. Never add `WHERE state = ...`
`SUM(openBalance)` for total AR. Filter `status <> 'Completed'` for outstanding
Always `WHERE days_on_shelf IS NOT NULL` when sorting/aggregating. ASC = fastest moving; DESC = stale

sample_rows.restrictions: Text, not DATE. Use `to_char()` for date arithmetic. Latest: `WHERE date = (SELECT MAX(date) FROM complete_market_scrapper_dataset)`. Last N days: `WHERE date >= to_char(CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 'N days', 'YYYY-MM-DD')`
